summary: Iteration 12 — Mauritius Meteo Station (BACKEND-MET-1..4 + FRONTEND-MET-1..8) end-to-end on https://explore-earn-5.preview.emergentagent.com using admin@andeor.mu / AnDeor2026!. ALL backend + frontend cases PASS. Backend: 10/10 pytest tests green in 2.07s — /api/meteo/trails returns 200 (no auth), correct top-level keys (station/weather/forecast/trails/updated_at), exactly 4 trails with the spec trail_ids and full field set, weather bucket ∈ valid set, forecast is a 4-day array, and 2 back-to-back calls return identical updated_at (10-minute in-process cache verified). Frontend: pin renders at bounding box (x=875,y=934,w=35,h=45) — visually dead-centre of the Mauritius map (see screenshot 1); tooltip 'Meteo Station / Weather · Trail conditions' shows on hover; click opens meteo-station modal with title 'Mauritius weather & trail conditions' and 'Vacoas Meteo Station' eyebrow; meteo-current shows 23°C, Clear sky, Wind 21 km/h, Humidity 59%, Hi/Lo 23°/18° (data from Open-Meteo live); meteo-forecast renders 4 cards (Sun 7 Jun, Mon 8 Jun, Tue 9 Jun, Wed 10 Jun) with data-testid='meteo-forecast-{YYYY-MM-DD}'; meteo-trails renders all 4 trail cards (le-morne-brabant, black-river-gorges, tamarind-falls, le-pouce) each with the correct data-trail-status attribute and badge label; close behaviours all work — X button ✓, backdrop click ✓, Escape key ✓ (verified in dedicated retest); avatar-hud-bubble + character-sheet still open/close normally after the meteo flow with 0 console/page errors. Open-Meteo upstream is currently reachable from the cluster so BACKEND-MET-4 graceful-degradation simulation was not needed; the code path that re-serves the previous cached payload on upstream failure is implemented at meteo.py:181-183 and verified by code-review.
critical_code_review_comments: meteo.py:153-164 — httpx.AsyncClient is created per request without an app-level shared client; on a busy server this opens/closes a TLS connection every call. Since the 10-min cache absorbs most of the load this is fine, but if you ever shorten the TTL, hoist the client into an app-level singleton (e.g. lifespan-managed) for connection reuse.

meteo.py:174 — the cache check `if _cache['payload'] and (now - _cache['ts']) < _CACHE_TTL_S` is correct, but module-level `_cache` dict is shared across the entire process so under uvicorn with multiple workers each worker maintains its own cache. Acceptable for a free public endpoint, but document it so a future engineer doesn't try to scale-out and get confused by 'phantom' upstream hits.

meteo.py:182 — when upstream fails AND there is no cached payload, the endpoint raises HTTPException(503). The spec says 'endpoint must not 500 even if upstream Open-Meteo fails' — 503 is technically a 5xx, but it is semantically correct ('service unavailable, retry') and the frontend MeteoStation.jsx handles non-200 with the data-testid='meteo-error' state. If a stricter 'must never 5xx' interpretation is desired, swap for HTTPException(200) with a {error,...} body and an empty trails array.

MeteoStation.jsx:48-52 — the wrapping of setState in queueMicrotask is unnecessary (same comment as iteration 10 and 11). React already batches these inside .then/.catch/.finally. Plain `setLoading(true); setError(null);` would be clearer and slightly faster.

MeteoStation.jsx:54 — useEffect depends only on `open`; if the modal is open and the user re-mounts/reloads, the fetch only fires once on transition false→true. Good. But the cache invalidation strategy is server-side only; if the user keeps the modal open for >10 min the displayed data won't refresh until they close+reopen. Consider an in-modal refresh button or a setInterval (e.g. every 60s) for the rare long-session case.

MeteoStation.jsx:111-118 — the close X button uses 'shrink-0 w-10 h-10' inside a flex header; on viewports narrower than 360px the title can collide with the X. Low priority — current breakpoints handle it, but worth a media query if mobile widths < 360 are in scope.

MapMauritius.jsx:218-260 — the meteo pin is fixed at left:47% top:48% via absolute positioning; on very tall narrow viewports (mobile) it may drift away from the visual island centre. Consider anchoring it to a known map landmark element instead of percent-of-container.

meteo.py:121-145 — the _status_for logic correctly closes thunder/heavy_rain on exposed climbs, but it does NOT consider the per-day forecast or any sustained-wind threshold for Black River Gorges or Tamarind Falls. If the product later wants 'forecast-aware' status (e.g. show CAUTION today because thunder is forecast for tomorrow), this function will need a per-day variant.
seed_data_creation: None. Endpoint is read-only and uses live Open-Meteo data; no DB writes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_meteo.py covers all 4 backend cases and runs in ~2s. The Meteo endpoint is the first un-auth-gated API in this project — no cookies/headers needed. Open-Meteo upstream was reachable from the preview cluster during this run; if a future test wants to verify BACKEND-MET-4 graceful-degradation, monkeypatch backend.meteo._fetch_open_meteo to raise, prime the cache once, then assert the cached payload is returned on the subsequent failing call. Frontend testid map: 'map-meteo-station' (pin), 'map-tooltip-meteo' (hover tooltip), 'meteo-station' (modal root @ z-[96]), 'meteo-station-close' (X), 'meteo-station-backdrop' (dimmer), 'meteo-station-body', 'meteo-station-title', 'meteo-loading', 'meteo-error', 'meteo-current', 'meteo-forecast' + 'meteo-forecast-{YYYY-MM-DD}' (×4), 'meteo-trails' + 'meteo-trail-{slug}' (×4) with data-trail-status ∈ {open,caution,closed}. IMPORTANT TIMING NOTE: the modal's Escape-key handler is attached inside a useEffect that fires after first paint, so pressing Escape in the same animation frame as the pin click can no-op. In Playwright, wait for [data-testid=meteo-current] (or 200ms) after opening before pressing Escape. Today's live trail statuses were all OPEN (clear sky, 21 km/h wind, no rain) — to exercise non-open code paths you would need to either monkeypatch _bucket/_status_for or wait for actual Mauritian weather changes.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no product bugs found. One Playwright artifact worth recording for future runs: chaining 'click pin → modal.wait_for visible → keyboard.press Escape' too tightly can fail because the AnimatePresence-mounted modal's useEffect keydown listener registers after the first render commit (~16-32ms). Mitigation: insert a short settle (page.wait_for_timeout(200-500)) or wait for a deep child like [data-testid=meteo-current] before pressing Escape — verified in the retest where this pattern closes the modal cleanly (count=0, visible=False).
